---
title: "We Will Be Jaguars: A Memoir of My People by Nemonte Nenquimo - Review"
---


This is an amazing memoir about an Indigenous childhood in the Amazon rain forest. I never fully understood the history of evangelical missionaries in Indigenous communities, but I was shocked by the extent to which they exploted these communities. As an environmental studies major, this gave me insight into history of BIg Oil in this areas, and gave me hope for climate change activism. Anyone from any background or major should read this book.
